title: Tangy Ethiopian Grape Nut Kik Alicha
description: Fresh, flaky hangover‑cure Ethiopian dish featuring grape nut cereal and char‑grilled vegetables, jain‑friendly and packed with tang.
instructions: In a saucepan, combine grape nuts and 2 cups of water and bring to a boil over medium heat.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 10 minutes until the mixture thickens.
While the mixture cooks, heat a grill pan and char the bell peppers and onions until slightly blackened.
Transfer the charred vegetables to a bowl, let them cool, then roughly chop them.
Stir the lemon juice, salt, thyme, cumin, and coconut oil into the simmering grape nut mixture.
Fold in the charred vegetables, chopped cilantro, and a pinch of black pepper, cooking for an additional 3 minutes until warmed through.
Serve the tangy kik alicha hot in bowls, garnished with extra cilantro if desired.
Enjoy as a revitalizing, hangover‑cure lunch that delivers fresh flavor and flaky texture.
